title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. lineata
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. lineata, also known as Lady's Mantle, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its broad, toothed leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including astringency, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce menstrual cramps, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Lady's Mantle include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating menopausal symptoms, while also supporting digestive health and immune function. Herbal preparations of Lady's Mantle can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
